items.headline: CIT Switches Lead Case as Atmus Withdraws from IEEPA Litigation
items.summary: Atmus Filtration filed notice of dismissal on April 6, ending its role as the lead IEEPA case. Euro-Nations Florida now serves as the new lead case, potentially resetting appeal deadlines and creating uncertainty about CAPE implementation timing. The government has until June 6 to appeal the CIT orders.

items.headline: CAPE System 85% Complete for Claim Portal as April 20 Launch Nears
items.summary: CBP reports the Claim Portal component is now 85% complete with other CAPE modules between 60-80% complete. Phase 1 will process approximately 63% of affected entries starting around April 20. Only 26,000 of 330,000 eligible importers have completed ACH enrollment, creating rejection risk for unprepared companies.

items.headline: 92% of IEEPA Importers Still Not ACH-Enrolled Before CAPE Launch
items.summary: With CAPE's late April launch imminent, only 26,664 of 330,000 eligible importers have completed required ACH electronic refund enrollment. CBP will reject refund claims from importers without proper ACE access and ACH setup, creating significant risk for unprepared companies.

items.headline: Phase 1 CAPE Limited to 63% of Entries, Complex Cases Deferred
items.summary: CBP confirms Phase 1 will only handle unliquidated entries and those liquidated within 80 days. Finally liquidated entries, reconciliation entries, and drawback claims excluded until future phases. No timeline provided for Phase 2 development to handle more complex scenarios.

items.headline: CIT Endorses CBP's Satisfactory Progress on CAPE Development
items.summary: Judge Eaton issued order confirming CBP is making satisfactory progress on all four CAPE components and is on track to meet the April 20 launch deadline. The court directed CBP to file a status report by April 14 with a closed conference scheduled for 3pm EDT that day.

items.headline: CBP Details 45-Day Processing Timeline for CAPE Refund Claims
items.summary: CBP filed detailed March 31 declaration stating it will process accepted refund requests within 45 days from acceptance, unless compliance concerns require additional review. The system will validate CSV file uploads and automatically recalculate duties without IEEPA provisions.
